title: Isolating human intestinal crypts from biopsies for organoid generation
author: Ran RZ Zhou
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcqsivwe

steps.title: Collect biopsies in PBS or culture media in 1
steps.instruction: Collect biopsies in PBS or culture media in 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Incubate all the tissues from each...
steps.instruction: Incubate all the tissues from each biopsy in 25mL of 8 mM EDTA/PBS in a 50 ml conical tube for 30 min, rocking (not shaking) at 4C. 4 °C 00:30:00

steps.title: Place one 100 um cell strainer...
steps.instruction: Place one 100 um cell strainer on each well of a 6-well plate. Pre-wet the cell strainer with 1mL culture media.

steps.title: Transfer biopsies from EDTA/PBS into 1-2 ml ADF
steps.instruction: Transfer biopsies from EDTA/PBS into 1-2 ml ADF.

steps.title: Using a customized P1000 aerosol resistant...
steps.instruction: Using a customized P1000 aerosol resistant tip, pipet up and down vigorously, making sure that the biopsy tissue goes in and out of the tip, but with some difficulty. This helps release the crypts. Customize the pipet tip: tips can be altered by cutting it to slightly smaller than the size of the largest biopsy, using a sterilized razor blade. Cut the pipet tip at a straight angle, not biased, to keep the hole as small as possible.

steps.title: Use the 100 um cell strainer...
steps.instruction: Use the 100 um cell strainer to filter the tissue and large pieces from the crypts. Collect the crypts into a 1.5 ml microfuge tube. Check to see whether enough crypts were released from the tissue on a microscope.

steps.title: Centrifuge the crypts at 300-400 g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the crypts at 300-400 g at 4C for 5 min in a tabletop swinging bucket centrifuge 300 x g, °C, 5 minutes 4 °C A microcentrifuge is not recommended here because it is a fixed angle and we want the pellet to be at the bottom of the tube, not on the side

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the crypts in pre-warmed and CO2-equilibrated human organoid media (complete ADF) containing growth factors

steps.title: Mix crypts with thawed matrigel in...
steps.instruction: Mix crypts with thawed matrigel in a ratio of 1:2 (cells:matrigel). Pipet up and down; avoid creating bubbles

steps.title: Plate crypt/matrigel mixture onto a pre-warmed...
steps.instruction: Plate crypt/matrigel mixture onto a pre-warmed 12- or 24-well tissue culture plate, 100 or 50 ul/well respectively.

steps.title: Incubate 45-60 min in 37C, 5%...
steps.instruction: Incubate 45-60 min in 37C, 5% CO2 incubator 37 °C 00:45:00, Incubation

steps.title: Add complete ADF into each well (24-well
steps.instruction: Add complete ADF into each well (24-well: 0.5 ml/well; 12-well: 1 ml/well)

steps.title: Feed organoids with complete ADF every other day
steps.instruction: Feed organoids with complete ADF every other day. Also add ciprofloxacin (10 ug/ml) to make sure that there are no remaining bacteria in your culture. Media is changed every other day. Organoids are split and expanded as needed, about once per week.

steps.title: Once biopsies received, wash 3 times...
steps.instruction: Once biopsies received, wash 3 times with 25 ml ice cold PBS each in 50 ml conical tubes, or until PBS looks clear. Biopsied tissues range from 6 to 20 mm3 in size. All the tissues biopsied from the same site are pooled for downstream procedures.

steps.title: Prechill PBS and 8mM EDTA in PBS on ice
steps.instruction: Prechill PBS and 8mM EDTA in PBS on ice. Pre-warm human organoid medium and 12-well/24-well plate in the tissue-culture incubator.
